title: Savory Polish Sardine Pilaf
description: A savory and fluffy Polish dish featuring sardines, perfect for a lazy Sunday. This low-fat-friendly recipe is a great way to enjoy a hearty meal without feeling guilty.

ingredients.name: dried parsley
ingredients.notes: fresh parsley can be used as substitute

ingredients.name: dried dill weed
ingredients.notes: fresh dill can be used as substitute

ingredients.name: salt
ingredients.notes: preferably sea salt or kosher salt

instructions: Heat a large skillet over medium heat and add chopped onion, carrot, and celery. Cook until the vegetables are tender, about 8 minutes.
Add the chopped sardines to the skillet and cook for an additional 2 minutes, breaking them up with a spoon as they cook.
Add the rice to the skillet and cook for 1-2 minutes, stirring constantly, until the rice is coated in the oil and slightly toasted.
Add the chicken broth, dried parsley, and dried dill weed to the skillet. Stir to combine and bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low, cover the skillet with a tight-fitting lid, and simmer for 18-20 minutes, or until the liquid is absorbed and the rice is tender.
Remove the skillet from the heat and let it rest, covered, for 5 minutes.
Fluff the pilaf with a fork and season with salt and black pepper to taste.
Serve the pilaf hot, garnished with chopped fresh parsley or dill if desired.
Refrigerate or freeze any leftovers for later use.
Reheat the pilaf gently before serving, adding a splash of chicken broth if it seems dry.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
